dialog.stepLabel: Arrive at the Hunter's Den and take part in the ceremony

items.text: May joy also be upon you, {PlayerName}.
The Sovereign is dead. Now we pause in our victory to honor the Hero of Heroes, and all those who stood by her side.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: I am Bruno, Master of Ceremonies for the occasion. I had hoped to greet you alongside Augusta.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: But she was called away... The Senate insists on wasting her time with their trifles.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: We wish to bestow upon you the honor of Rosebearer. Roses symbolize our glory and triumph, to be shared with all who are gathered here.

options.text: Rosebearer?

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: Yes, it's a ceremonial role. Don't worry, it's pretty simple, and you won't be handing out too many.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: At the height of the celebration, everyone will toss their petals into the air. We will all shower in the flowers.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: Your task will be to distribute the petals beforehand. You have been chosen by the people, for you are held in the highest regard.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: The people of Septimont gave their all on the battlefield. Now, after fighting side by side, we'll share honey and wine beneath a rain of petals.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: You are our first non-local Rosebearer. But I believe you were already chosen the moment you cut through the Dark Tide's miasma. Without you, the Sovereign's defeat would not have been possible.

options.text: Eh? You're saying I did that?

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: Yes... Oh, I hope you won't decline. This is not only Augusta's wish, it's the people's too.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: When Augusta arrives, she'll join the other Rosebearers. I'm sure those who partook in the hunt will be honored to receive petals from you.

options.text: I'll give this local tradition a shot.
It would be my honor.
Sounds like a good way to soak in the atmosphere.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: Very well, I'll leave it in your capable hands.

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: As for who you choose and how, trust your instincts. It's just like a toast, or a Priestess's oration, all part of the celebration. No need to overthink it. Now, enjoy!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: {PlayerName}, you've come.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Bruno has already filled you in on the Rosebearer matter, yes? How are the celebrations treating you?

options.text: I have something more pressing to discuss with you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Augusta, do you remember Iuno?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Iuno?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Yes, she was often by your side. The one with the bow like the moon. She joined our hunt for the Sovereign...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Everyone has forgotten... No, that's not it. Everyone says they've never even seen her before.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But you, you remember her. You remember everything that happened. You are certain she is real and cannot exist in your memories alone.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I know what you're feeling, because I've felt it too.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Dark Tide's frequencies twisted my perception as well. Made me see things that weren't real.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Sovereign's whispers dogged me for decades, tempting me to accept its gift.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But that's all in the past. I know now that to find peace, I must see through the deception, fight it, and conquer it.

options.text: How can you be sure it's deception?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Because of my sword. It reflects everything that happened in Murmurstown.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Existence always leaves a trace. Beyond feeling, memory, and perception, is there anything that can prove this "Iuno" isn't an illusion?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: After this, I shall likely sleep like I've never slept before.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Say, I heard the Montelli family imported some device that aids sleep. It's all the rage... Augusta, I trust you will acquire one for me? I wish to try it myself.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Scented candles are fine, though the scent of the forest is most pleasing. As for pillows and bedding...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: That's enough. I allow requests... but only three.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Hmph. Apologies, but I need my rest if I'm to be at full power. Do bear with it. It's for your own good, after all.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: And you, {PlayerName}? Were I to indulge in a bit of selfishness, you'd forgive me, wouldn't you?

options.text: Saying that in itself is rather selfish.
Depends on how selfish you get.
No, I wouldn't.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Oh? Didn't think the great "Unwritten {PlayerName}" would be so stingy.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Well, lucky for you, I'm feeling generous today. Now, what brings you to me?

options.text: You can have this back.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Really? You traveled all this way just to return what I gave you, is that it?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Was it truly such a burden for you?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: If one day this bracelet becomes meaningless to anyone else, I hope you will remain the exception, Unwritten One.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: But... perhaps in that distant future, forgetting might prove far easier than remembering. Never mind. In any case, you are not to return it.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: It's something I've carried with me ever since I was bestowed the title of Priestess. Keep it for me, if only as a keepsake.

options.text: For how long must I keep it?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Well, until we meet again, I suppose.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This bracelet belongs to Iuno.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Quite the unique design. Like a crescent moon. But still... I'm sorry.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I wish I could offer you the answer you seek, but to me, it's just an ordinary bracelet. Nothing about it stands out.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: When you mentioned her name, I tried to recall any trace of her in my memories.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But there is nothing. What has passed is clear... and she feels like an extra piece to a puzzle, one that doesn't quite fit.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: How can this be? You spent the most time with her.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: {PlayerName}, wait...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: What... did you see?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Iuno.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Of course, only you would be the exception.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So you've known this all along...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: A death that shouldn't have happened, traded for an unforetold victory. A fair deal.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Now... I am but a fading sliver of moonlight.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'm sorry, but...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Even I fear being...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Forgotten...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey, {PlayerName}, you alright? You've been standing here for ages.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: So Iuno's really gone? Just like that?

options.text: You still remember her too?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Duh! Even though I was snoozing on and off... I still remember everything that went down, crystal clear!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You told me the first time you met Iuno was in the arena, right? She scared the toga off that other team with just a few words. Intimidating stuff. But when I actually met her, she was...

options.text: She only seems like she has an attitude.
She's unexpectedly open and straightforward.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yeah, totally! We had so much done with her during the hunt trip.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: She even invited us to Tetragon Temple to check out the murals. Oh, and we caught that giant blue spiky TD, which led us to that big bad stinky thing...

options.text: We faced the Sovereign's ambush together, too.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yep, and I'm pretty sure she was the one who took that Moon Bow and blasted the Dark Tide wide open! Not you, not anyone else!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: And that bracelet, she gave it to us to lure out that nasty big fella... Even asked us to keep it for her...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: So it doesn't matter whatever anyone thinks, or... or what happened to her. You remember her, and I do too! It wasn't some made-up crap!

options.text: Iuno said she traded her life for our victory...
Iuno said only I would be the exception...
Iuno said she didn't want to be forgotten...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Iuno once said she glimpsed the end, where she would be the last one standing. But now, this end never came... and Iuno is dead.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Is this the cost of changing destiny? Did she know she'd disappear, but accepted it anyway?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Why am I special? Why has everyone forgotten everything about Iuno except me?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: But if I'm the exception, if I'm the only one who still remembers... does that mean there's something I can do about it?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: She didn't even get to finish her sentence. What was she thinking? Why did she do it... If this is the end, maybe I'll never know.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yeah, I know what you mean... Like, we didn't even get to have a proper goodbye!

options.text: I can't accept this as the end.
I need to know how it all happened.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I don't care if Iuno foresaw her fate and chose this path for herself.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: All I know is I won't let it end like this. Not with her fading into oblivion.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Why did Iuno choose this? Did she truly believe this was the better ending?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'm the one she entrusted with her bracelet. The exception. So it's on me to find the answer.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Like Augusta said, everything that exists leaves a trace. Since she's a Priestess, I'm sure to find traces of her in Tetragon Temple.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: A lead could be the breakthrough I need.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: That's the spirit! Look at ya, {PlayerName}, snapping out of that funk! Knew you'd be back at it soon enough!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I still got some kinks to work out before I'm in my Super Echo Abby form, but you can bet I'll be here...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Remembering everything that happens. We'll figure something out, whatever it takes! Even when I'm just riding along, I'm in this with you, pal.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Looks like the oration they mentioned.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Huh… I thought for sure it'd be Iuno at an event like this.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Eh, well. Guess I'll catch her later.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: ...Yes, I fear that until Grandmother Lillibet finds a gifted successor, we shall not glimpse further into <ano= Septimont's Auguries>Septimont's future</ano>.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: By the light and fate above us, we are but apprentices carving insights into stone, passing their words along.

options.text: (Gifted successor? Shouldn't it just be Iuno?)
(Is Iuno no longer serving as Priestess?)

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Once, fate flowed like a thread. It guided reverent seekers to glimpse Septimont's end...

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Under the Tide's surge, all was overturned. No soul was spared.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Until the Unwritten One arrived from afar to join forces with Augusta.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: With the Corrosaurus as bait, {Male=he;Female=she} cut through the Dark Tide's miasma to reveal the truth.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: With the False Sovereign's blood, a new ruler was crowned. The prophesied end was averted, thanks to Augusta and the Unwritten One.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: From now on, fear no longer grips me. The Dark Tide retreats into the forgotten night, and countless souls drink from pure waters again. The new Hero of Heroes, Augusta, shall lead Septimont into a new age.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Thus, the present now aligns with the prophecy Grandmother Lillibet once foresaw in Septimont's Auguries.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Prophecy of the end, and how we managed to lure the Sovereign out... The order and the story are right, but something's missing.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You speak of me and Augusta, but what of Iuno?

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Who is... Iuno?

options.text: Iuno is Iuno, what do you mean?
Is this supposed to be a joke?

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Unwritten One, you leave me baffled.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Are you suggesting that my recounting missed... someone named Iuno? I don't find the name... familiar.

options.text: You're not just forgetting her in your story.
You speak like you don't know her at all.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Hmm. I should know this... Iuno, you speak of, yes?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Iuno is a Priestess, just like you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: More than that, she wielded the Moon Bow and fought alongside me and Augusta in the hunt. We defeated the Sovereign together... Don't you all remember?

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Impossible. As a Priestess myself, I can tell you with certainty that none among us bear that name.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: And, I must remind you, we rarely take part in hunts. Our place is with the <ano=Sacred Flames>flames</ano> in Tetragon Temple.

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: The hunt for the Sovereign involved many. Perhaps you are mistaken and confused her for someone else?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: No, this can't be.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I don't know what a Priestess is supposed to be, but I do know what Iuno is like. And I'm certain... she's a Priestess.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You mentioned cutting through the Dark Tide's miasma. Tell me, if Iuno wasn't there, then who shot the Moon Arrow?

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Who shot the Moon Arrow...? What do you mean? You were the one who dispelled the miasma.

options.text: It was... me?

items.speaker: Serene Priestess
items.text: Yes. On the cliffside, you struck with your sword to cut through the Dark Tide. Don't you remember, Unwritten One?

items.speaker: Valiant Gladiator
items.text: Come on over! Toast!

items.speaker: Outspoken Gladiator
items.text: Let's drink till we drop!

items.speaker: Worried Noble
items.text: We should just live in the moment and enjoy what we have now...

items.speaker: Helpful Noble
items.text: That's more like it!

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: Come, triumphant rain of petals...

items.speaker: Ceremonial Officer Bruno
items.text: Make it even grander...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Didn't you tell me we would meet at the very end? Then why can't I find you...)

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I could hear your voice from a distance, "Iuno, Iuno!" Wasn't that it?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Seems like you're in an awful hurry to find someone.

options.text: You little... Do you know how worried I was?
Back to cracking jokes. Guess we're good now.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I thought you'd like this, us slipping back into our old, familiar rhythm... Now you know I'm still me. And yes, I really am back.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Exactly. See? I come right away and stand before you, in the flesh.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: It's just like you saw at the end of the Chaos. Three years ago, at the end of the spring basin swallowed by the Dark Tide, I, as a Priestess, made my choice.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I was so done with just being dragged along by the moon's whims... So I defied fate, severed the path, and became the arrow that struck at destiny itself.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: After that, I lost the ability to see clear visions of the future, but in return, I gained the power to anchor the Dark Tide's creations.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: With that, the last prophecy I saw changed slightly. In a squad doomed to fall, one survived—Harelius.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: If I can do it once, then I can do it a second time, then a third... Perhaps one day, I can overturn more destined futures.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Since fate didn't prepare a Hero of Heroes, we decided to eliminate the need for one by taking on the disaster at its source—the Sovereign and the Paradise.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: This is the plan Augusta and I made, though I did hide the teeny tiny cost.

options.text: Your disappearance.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Anyway, all the Septimontians who were supposed to die are still alive. The end I once foresaw has changed. The meaning of this has already transcended my own existence.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: So then, even if every trace of my "existence" was erased, who can say that the altered fate I left behind isn't proof of my existence?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: More importantly, I had the privilege of standing by your side to witness what lies beyond the end. The void. An unwritten... void. Perhaps it signifies the unimaginable possibilities yet to come.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Just like you, bringing unknown possibilities to Septimont... and to me.

options.text: Let's make sure there's no next time.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Why, sacrificing myself? Or just trying something I'm not sure about?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Come now, you know me... I follow my whims. And the more I trust someone, the harder it is to keep my impulses in check.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'm not one to make promises I can't keep, but I'll promise you this... if there is a next time, you'll be the first to know.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: And when I do, you'll get the full story. No holding back.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I've run into a few friends since being back. Not one of them recognized me.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Guess the memory of the Iuno they once knew is just... the price we had to pay.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: But, at least... you still remember me. You pulled me from the Chaos and brought me back to existence. That's enough.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Oh, I'm certain Augusta and I, along with the rest of them, will be thick as thieves again, this time creating new futures. You really shouldn't underestimate my charm!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Oh yes, and this bracelet...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: This is the second time I've given this to you. Never mind the first time, but I wouldn't refuse a second time, if I were you.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Being the second time means there's been a change... Now, it's a testament to our secret little interactions. I've Anchored the world, and you, you've Anchored me.

options.text: (Accept the bracelet and put it on.)
(Accept the bracelet and put it away.)

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: As for any matter pertaining to me... What you saw in the Chaos and what transpired, do not breathe a word of it to another soul. Understand?

options.text: Feeling bashful all of a sudden?
It will be our little secret then.
That so? I'll keep it in mind.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Hmph, bashful? Hardly! If you blurt it out, it would cause quite a stir. All that explaining would be too much hassle.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Besides, to them, I am... quite the anomaly. If the truth came out, the first reaction would be shock.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: You think so too? Okay. Good. I'm most relieved.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: You think so too? Okay. Good. I'm most relieved.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Well, then again, this is a perfect opportunity to start anew.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: In Septimont, it would be a crime to miss the height of the celebration.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: After fighting side by side, we share honey and wine under a rain of petals, drinking to our hearts' content... Surely you've heard of this little tradition of ours, haven't you, Unwritten One?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Come on, let's go. Together... to be drenched in those rose petals that smell of glory and triumph.

items.speaker: Voice in the Chaos
items.text: Endlessly cycling... shattering futures... Is this Chaos?

items.speaker: Voice in the Chaos
items.text: Unsalvageable, fractured into pieces... This is Chaos!

items.speaker: Voice in the Chaos
items.text: Countless possibilities, both true and not true... This is Chaos...

items.speaker: Voice in the Chaos
items.text: ...Until every Anchor is revealed, laid bare in the light.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: My apologies. I should've been here to receive you, but some Priestesses ran into some trouble... I had to step away.

options.text: What trouble?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Simply put, aside from what's already been verified, we can no longer see any other of Septimont's Auguries.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: The full scope has always been out of reach, but this feeling of being blocked by an impenetrable white barrier is something I've never encountered. It's as if there's nothing there at all.

options.text: All the Priestesses' abilities have failed?
Can you still make prophecies?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Well, there is a silver lining. Septimont's Auguries concern Septimont's future, but as for smaller, individual futures... we can still see those.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: For instance, I saw Avidius making a commotion not long ago.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: But let's talk about why you are here first. Augusta mentioned your situation and said you might come looking for me. You seek help in finding a Priestess by the name of Iuno.

options.text: Iuno once lived and breathed in this world.
Everyone's forgotten her. Except for me.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: I wish she were a figment of your imagination. A product of your proximity to the Dark Tide. That would make things much simpler.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Because I did find a record of a Priestess named Iuno.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: But according to the record, she died three years ago. Buried where the Dark Tide swallowed the spring basin.

options.text: How did she die? And why?
A death that shouldn't have happened. Is that a coincidence...

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: The record didn't say, but...

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: You must understand, "death" and "disappearance" are two different concepts.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: If Iuno truly died three years ago, then she couldn't have been your companion three years later, let alone have existed up until today.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: In other words, if Iuno disappeared today and was erased from the memories of Augusta, myself, and everyone else, then the death three years ago must have been a fabrication.

options.text: Can you say that again, but in simple terms?
What does it mean if her death was a fabrication?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: It means her death three years ago and her disappearance today cannot both be true.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: If someone has truly disappeared from this world, do they need a fabricated death? Likewise, when someone dies, they no longer exist in a way that others can feel. These two causal chains should be clear, but they crossed paths with you, the unknowable void.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Although I hate to admit it, Avidius was right... Since you still remember Iuno and thus hold onto that last connection between her and the world, you've essentially "anchored" her.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: This has caused the causal chain to become tangled. The ending is still unwritten. Right now, she is but a specter, stuck reliving her past in the Chaos between life and death, between existence and non-existence.

options.text: And I can choose which causal chain to keep.
What if I enter the Chaos myself...

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: In theory, yes. You could forget her, let her disappear entirely. Or, you could forget her and let her die for good.

options.text: Is there really no other way?
What if neither of those work for me?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: I believe insight into fate is not to tempt one's defiance. It will move according to its intended end.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Besides, it's already granted you a rare gift—two options. More than most ever get.

options.text: Then I'll make my own deal with fate.
I refuse to let fate dictate my choices.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: So you wish to use yourself as a vessel to anchor the memories, feelings, and connections with Iuno in the Chaos, manifesting her back into existence?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Anchor, manifest, whatever you want to call it. All I know is Iuno's not gone because of me. So why shouldn't I be able to bring her back?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: If I can tangle the causal chain, why can't I create a new possibility?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Your thinking is... bold, to say the least.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: But do you really understand? Chaos isn't just disorder. It's the aggregation of all things. A web of cause and effect, the convergence of infinite possibilities. If you end up lost within it, you risk being consumed, never to return.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Even ones such as we, guided by fate, can only pull small insights from certain shores of destiny. What if you enter only to find that you cannot bring Iuno back?

options.text: But you didn't say I can't enter.
So just tell me how to do it.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Chaos isn't a place in the usual sense. The key to entering isn't about where you are, but what's happening and what you do in that moment.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: I suspect that three years ago, this Priestess must have done something that defied fate, which set the course for her inevitable disappearance.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: If you can get close to that inescapable knot in the causal chain, maybe you'll find a way in.

options.text: Then I'll go to the place where the record said she died.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Their accounts are all true, except when it comes to Iuno. How they react to her name is completely wrong.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Everything she did, they attribute to me. They're not just forgetting. It's as if Iuno never existed to them at all.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Could this be possible? This distortion in perception? Is it a remnant of the Sovereign's power? Or is this the work of the Fractsidus again?)

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: I heard what you said to the Priestess, {PlayerName}.

options.text: You remember Iuno?

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: No... I've never even heard of her.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: You should know, I've been stationed in this camp longer than most. Memory ain't bad, either.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: Been thinking... Maybe the Dark Tide's crawled into your head. Could be you're suffering from dissociation, a side effect of all that exposure.

options.text: Dissociation?

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: Word is, people who get too close end up seeing things. Hallucinations, delusions, the works.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: Some of 'em go as far as thinking they see the dead. Or worse, some start calling themselves Tacet Discords.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: The Sovereign was built from the Dark Tide itself. Who knows what it swallowed before it came for us? Could be worse than what you're used to. You must be in its grip.

options.text: Do you mean to say I'm the crazy one?
I never heard of this dissociation side effect before.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: Ah, don't get too worked up. It ain't that bad, not yet anyway.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: See, the thing is, it's not like there's one solid name for it. Happens in all kinds of ways, different for each person. And…

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: At any rate, dissociation is just a problem in perception.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: Some folks let it eat at them for life. Others know what's real and what ain't. They keep away from the Dark Tide, and before long they start feeling alright.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: How are you feeling? You need to rest up a bit? ...Oh, looks like Augusta's finally here.

items.speaker: Veteran Elder
items.text: She's been through it too, faced the Sovereign just like you. If you're still doubting, maybe talk to her. Could help.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: We're here.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: This is where the record says she died, three years ago.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Nothing here... Well, that's okay. You tried, {PlayerName}.

options.text: No. We've already come this far.
Did we take the wrong path?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Guided by the light of fate, we walk the web of futures. Many seek prophecies to follow or to break free from... all in pursuit of harmonious completeness.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: I've never doubted your judgment of Iuno. But if, as you say, she knew what would happen and still chose this path... could it be that this was the closest she could get to true completeness?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: If you refuse this ending, how can you be certain that you can create a better one?

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: A life with all its experiences and feelings, shredded, scattered, and thrown into the realm of Chaos... What you find there may not be beautiful. It will be filled with pain and emptiness.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Even if you do find a way in, your time together may be brief.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: With so much uncertainty, do you still feel that you must go?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: If there were any doubt, now... Now I'm sure.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Wait, you—

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I'm not rejecting the ending Iuno wrote. I just want to fix the regret she left behind.

options.text: This is not about her, but me.
Because this ending wasn't fair.
Because I don't want her to go like this.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This isn't the ending I'm happy with, that's all.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: An unnecessary death traded for an unforetold victory... She gave everything for Septimont only to be forgotten.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That's a fate no one should suffer. Not her, not anyone.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We've only just begun our story. The future I want... is one where she's there with all of us.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Forgetting may be easier than remembering. But let that be the choice of someone alive, not one who's already gone.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Right now I... don't want to lose anything I still have.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: The secret to anchoring, lies in connection and its manifestation.

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: If you can find what remains of her in the Chaos, and she responds to you...

items.speaker: Lillibet
items.text: Then follow her guidance. For that will be the only fragile rope connecting you, her, and this world.
